input.user_input: Insert a mutual confidentiality clause after Section 5 of the consulting agreement. It should cover both parties and last 3 years after termination.

actual:
I do not currently have access to your consulting agreement. If you upload the document, I can insert the clause directly after Section 5 and automatically update the numbering and cross-references for the rest of the agreement. 

In the meantime, here is a standard mutual confidentiality clause drafted to your specifications (covering both parties with a 3-year post-termination survival period) that you can use:

**[6]. Mutual Confidentiality**

**[6].1 Definition of Confidential Information.** "Confidential Information" means any non-public information disclosed by either party (the "Disclosing Party") to the other party (the "Receiving Party"), whether disclosed orally, in writing, or by inspection of tangible objects, that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential given the nature of the information and the circumstances of disclosure. 

**[6].2 Obligations.** The Receiving Party agrees to: (a) hold the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information in strict confidence; (b) protect the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information using the same degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature, but not less than reasonable care; (c) not disclose such Confidential Information to any third party without the Disclosing Party's prior written consent; and (d) not use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than to perform its obligations or exercise its rights under this Agreement.

**[6].3 Exclusions.** The obligations under this Section shall not apply to any information that: (a) is or becomes generally available to the public through no fault or
